title: AcyclicNonCollidersBlockable
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem acyclic_non_colliders_blockable
    (G : CDMG Node) (hG : G.IsAcyclic)
    {u v : Node} (π : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) :
    π.IsNonCollider k → π.IsBlockableNonCollider k
-- claim_3_20 -- end statement
:= by
  -- The proof translates the verified TeX proof at
  -- `tex/claim_3_20_proof_AcyclicNonCollidersBlockable.tex`.
  --
  -- Unfolding `IsBlockableNonCollider = IsNonCollider ∧
  -- ¬ IsUnblockableNonCollider`, the implication reduces to: assume
  -- the non-collider hypothesis (B1) and ¬ unblockable (B2).  Clause
  -- (B1) is the hypothesis; for (B2) we assume `hu : IsUnblockable…`
  -- and derive `False`.
  --
  -- Once `hu` is in scope, `IsUnblockableNonCollider`'s clauses force
  -- the position to be strictly interior (`1 ≤ k` from clause (ii)
  -- and `k + 1 ≤ π.length` from the `π.vertices[k + 1]? = some …`
  -- conjunct of clause (iii)).  So the end-position case of the TeX
  -- proof never arises: destructuring `hu` immediately places us in
  -- the interior case.  From there the TeX proof's Step 2.1–2.5
  -- cascade goes through:
  --
  --   * Step 2.1: from `IsNonCollider`'s `ah_π(k) ≤ 1` clause (= `¬
  --     IsCollider k`), one of `a_{k - 1}` / `a_k` is not into `v_k`.
  --   * Step 2.2: by the `WalkStep` relation at that slot, the
  --     "not-into" forces a directed walk-edge `(v_k, w) ∈ E` whose
  --     other endpoint `w` is on `π`.
  --   * Step 2.3: the unblockable implication at that slot gives
  --     `w ∈ Sc^G(v_k) ⊆ Anc^G(v_k)`, supplying a directed walk
  --     `ρ : Walk G w v_k`.
  --   * Step 2.4: prepending the single edge `(v_k, w)` to `ρ`
  --     yields a directed walk `v_k ⤳ v_k` of length `≥ 1`.
  --   * Step 2.5: contradicts `hG : G.IsAcyclic`.
  intro hk_nc
  refine ⟨hk_nc, ?_⟩
  intro hu
  obtain ⟨_, hk_pos, vkm1, vk, vkp1,
          hv_km1, hv_k, hv_kp1, h_km1_impl, h_k_impl⟩ := hu
  -- Extract the walk-edge at index `k` and the corresponding
  -- `WalkStep` relation, named in terms of `vk` and `vkp1`.
  obtain ⟨a_k, he_k, hWS_k⟩ := Walk.walkStep_at_vertices π hv_k hv_kp1
  -- Extract the walk-edge at index `k - 1` (and its `WalkStep`
  -- relation `G.WalkStep vkm1 a_km1 vk`).  We need the vertex
  -- Option-membership at position `(k - 1) + 1 = k`; rewrite `hv_k`.
  have hk_succ : k - 1 + 1 = k := Nat.sub_add_cancel hk_pos
  have hv_k' : π.vertices[k - 1 + 1]? = some vk := by rw [hk_succ]; exact hv_k
  obtain ⟨a_km1, he_km1, hWS_km1⟩ := Walk.walkStep_at_vertices π hv_km1 hv_k'
  -- Now `hWS_km1 : G.WalkStep vkm1 a_km1 vk` and
  --     `hWS_k   : G.WalkStep vk a_k vkp1`,
  -- with `he_km1 : π.edges[k - 1]? = some a_km1` and
  --     `he_k   : π.edges[k]? = some a_k`.
  -- Use `¬ IsCollider` to derive `¬ G.into vk a_km1 ∨ ¬ G.into vk a_k`.
  obtain ⟨_, h_notCol⟩ := hk_nc
  have h_notBothInto : ¬ (G.into vk a_km1 ∧ G.into vk a_k) := by
    rintro ⟨h1, h2⟩
    exact h_notCol ⟨hk_pos, vk, a_km1, a_k, hv_k, he_km1, he_k, h1, h2⟩
  -- Case-split on which walk-incident edge fails the "into" predicate.
  rcases not_and_or.mp h_notBothInto with hni_km1 | hni_k
  · -- ===== Case A: `¬ G.into vk a_km1`. =====
    -- The WalkStep at `k - 1` is `G.WalkStep vkm1 a_km1 vk`, with the
    -- forward writing `a_km1 = (vkm1, vk)` forcing `into` (via E or L
    -- clause).  Hence the only way for `¬ into` is the backward
    -- writing `a_km1 = (vk, vkm1) ∧ a_km1 ∈ E` — the desired outgoing
    -- walk-edge of `v_k`.
    rcases hWS_km1 with ⟨ha_eq, ha_EL⟩ | ⟨ha_eq, ha_E⟩
    · -- Forward writing: contradicts `¬ G.into vk a_km1`.
      exfalso; apply hni_km1
      rcases ha_EL with ha_E | ha_L
      · left
        refine ⟨ha_E, ?_⟩
        rw [ha_eq]
      · right
        refine ⟨ha_L, ?_⟩
        right; rw [ha_eq]
    · -- Backward writing: `a_km1 = (vk, vkm1) ∧ a_km1 ∈ G.E`.
      have h_edge_in_E : (vk, vkm1) ∈ G.E := ha_eq ▸ ha_E
      have he_km1_eq : π.edges[k - 1]? = some (vk, vkm1) := ha_eq ▸ he_km1
      have h_vkm1_Sc : vkm1 ∈ G.Sc vk := h_km1_impl ⟨he_km1_eq, h_edge_in_E⟩
      have h_vkm1_Anc : vkm1 ∈ G.Anc vk := h_vkm1_Sc.1
      obtain ⟨_, ρ, h_ρ_dir⟩ := h_vkm1_Anc
      -- Build the cycle by prepending the single edge `(vk, vkm1)` to ρ.
      have hstep : G.WalkStep vk (vk, vkm1) vkm1 := Or.inl ⟨rfl, Or.inl h_edge_in_E⟩
      let ρ_tilde : Walk G vk vk := Walk.cons vkm1 (vk, vkm1) hstep ρ
      have h_ρt_dir : ρ_tilde.IsDirectedWalk := ⟨rfl, h_edge_in_E, h_ρ_dir⟩
      have h_ρt_len : ρ_tilde.length ≥ 1 := by
        change ρ.length + 1 ≥ 1; omega
      have h_vk_in_G : vk ∈ G := (G.hE_subset h_edge_in_E).1
      exact hG vk h_vk_in_G ⟨ρ_tilde, h_ρt_dir, h_ρt_len⟩
  · -- ===== Case B: `¬ G.into vk a_k`. =====
    -- Symmetrically: the WalkStep at `k` has the backward writing
    -- `a_k = (vkp1, vk) ∈ E` forcing `into v_k` (via E-clause), so
    -- the only way for `¬ into` is the forward writing
    -- `a_k = (vk, vkp1)` with `a_k ∈ E` (taking `a_k ∈ L` would
    -- force `into` via the L-clause).
    rcases hWS_k with ⟨ha_eq, ha_EL⟩ | ⟨ha_eq, ha_E⟩
    · rcases ha_EL with ha_E | ha_L
      · -- `a_k = (vk, vkp1) ∧ a_k ∈ E`: desired outgoing walk-edge.
        have h_edge_in_E : (vk, vkp1) ∈ G.E := ha_eq ▸ ha_E
        have he_k_eq : π.edges[k]? = some (vk, vkp1) := ha_eq ▸ he_k
        have h_vkp1_Sc : vkp1 ∈ G.Sc vk := h_k_impl ⟨he_k_eq, h_edge_in_E⟩
        have h_vkp1_Anc : vkp1 ∈ G.Anc vk := h_vkp1_Sc.1
        obtain ⟨_, ρ, h_ρ_dir⟩ := h_vkp1_Anc
        have hstep : G.WalkStep vk (vk, vkp1) vkp1 := Or.inl ⟨rfl, Or.inl h_edge_in_E⟩
        let ρ_tilde : Walk G vk vk := Walk.cons vkp1 (vk, vkp1) hstep ρ
        have h_ρt_dir : ρ_tilde.IsDirectedWalk := ⟨rfl, h_edge_in_E, h_ρ_dir⟩
        have h_ρt_len : ρ_tilde.length ≥ 1 := by
          change ρ.length + 1 ≥ 1; omega
        have h_vk_in_G : vk ∈ G := (G.hE_subset h_edge_in_E).1
        exact hG vk h_vk_in_G ⟨ρ_tilde, h_ρt_dir, h_ρt_len⟩
      · -- `a_k = (vk, vkp1) ∈ L`: contradicts `¬ G.into vk a_k` via L-clause.
        exfalso; apply hni_k
        right
        refine ⟨ha_L, ?_⟩
        left; rw [ha_eq]
    · -- Backward writing `a_k = (vkp1, vk) ∈ E`: contradicts `¬ into` via E-clause.
      exfalso; apply hni_k
      left
      refine ⟨ha_E, ?_⟩
      rw [ha_eq]
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem acyclic_non_colliders_blockable
    (G : CDMG Node) (hG : G.IsAcyclic)
    {u v : Node} (π : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ) :
    π.IsNonCollider k → π.IsBlockableNonCollider k

:= by

  intro hk_nc
  refine ⟨hk_nc, ?_⟩
  intro hu
  obtain ⟨_, hk_pos, vkm1, vk, vkp1,
          hv_km1, hv_k, hv_kp1, h_km1_impl, h_k_impl⟩ := hu

  obtain ⟨a_k, he_k, hWS_k⟩ := Walk.walkStep_at_vertices π hv_k hv_kp1

  have hk_succ : k - 1 + 1 = k := Nat.sub_add_cancel hk_pos
  have hv_k' : π.vertices[k - 1 + 1]? = some vk := by rw [hk_succ]; exact hv_k
  obtain ⟨a_km1, he_km1, hWS_km1⟩ := Walk.walkStep_at_vertices π hv_km1 hv_k'

  obtain ⟨_, h_notCol⟩ := hk_nc
  have h_notBothInto : ¬ (G.into vk a_km1 ∧ G.into vk a_k) := by
    rintro ⟨h1, h2⟩
    exact h_notCol ⟨hk_pos, vk, a_km1, a_k, hv_k, he_km1, he_k, h1, h2⟩

  rcases not_and_or.mp h_notBothInto with hni_km1 | hni_k
  ·

    rcases hWS_km1 with ⟨ha_eq, ha_EL⟩ | ⟨ha_eq, ha_E⟩
    ·
      exfalso; apply hni_km1
      rcases ha_EL with ha_E | ha_L
      · left
        refine ⟨ha_E, ?_⟩
        rw [ha_eq]
      · right
        refine ⟨ha_L, ?_⟩
        right; rw [ha_eq]
    ·
      have h_edge_in_E : (vk, vkm1) ∈ G.E := ha_eq ▸ ha_E
      have he_km1_eq : π.edges[k - 1]? = some (vk, vkm1) := ha_eq ▸ he_km1
      have h_vkm1_Sc : vkm1 ∈ G.Sc vk := h_km1_impl ⟨he_km1_eq, h_edge_in_E⟩
      have h_vkm1_Anc : vkm1 ∈ G.Anc vk := h_vkm1_Sc.1
      obtain ⟨_, ρ, h_ρ_dir⟩ := h_vkm1_Anc

      have hstep : G.WalkStep vk (vk, vkm1) vkm1 := Or.inl ⟨rfl, Or.inl h_edge_in_E⟩
      let ρ_tilde : Walk G vk vk := Walk.cons vkm1 (vk, vkm1) hstep ρ
      have h_ρt_dir : ρ_tilde.IsDirectedWalk := ⟨rfl, h_edge_in_E, h_ρ_dir⟩
      have h_ρt_len : ρ_tilde.length ≥ 1 := by
        change ρ.length + 1 ≥ 1; omega
      have h_vk_in_G : vk ∈ G := (G.hE_subset h_edge_in_E).1
      exact hG vk h_vk_in_G ⟨ρ_tilde, h_ρt_dir, h_ρt_len⟩
  ·

    rcases hWS_k with ⟨ha_eq, ha_EL⟩ | ⟨ha_eq, ha_E⟩
    · rcases ha_EL with ha_E | ha_L
      ·
        have h_edge_in_E : (vk, vkp1) ∈ G.E := ha_eq ▸ ha_E
        have he_k_eq : π.edges[k]? = some (vk, vkp1) := ha_eq ▸ he_k
        have h_vkp1_Sc : vkp1 ∈ G.Sc vk := h_k_impl ⟨he_k_eq, h_edge_in_E⟩
        have h_vkp1_Anc : vkp1 ∈ G.Anc vk := h_vkp1_Sc.1
        obtain ⟨_, ρ, h_ρ_dir⟩ := h_vkp1_Anc
        have hstep : G.WalkStep vk (vk, vkp1) vkp1 := Or.inl ⟨rfl, Or.inl h_edge_in_E⟩
        let ρ_tilde : Walk G vk vk := Walk.cons vkp1 (vk, vkp1) hstep ρ
        have h_ρt_dir : ρ_tilde.IsDirectedWalk := ⟨rfl, h_edge_in_E, h_ρ_dir⟩
        have h_ρt_len : ρ_tilde.length ≥ 1 := by
          change ρ.length + 1 ≥ 1; omega
        have h_vk_in_G : vk ∈ G := (G.hE_subset h_edge_in_E).1
        exact hG vk h_vk_in_G ⟨ρ_tilde, h_ρt_dir, h_ρt_len⟩
      ·
        exfalso; apply hni_k
        right
        refine ⟨ha_L, ?_⟩
        left; rw [ha_eq]
    ·
      exfalso; apply hni_k
      left
      refine ⟨ha_E, ?_⟩
      rw [ha_eq]
lean_explanation:
The theorem `acyclic_non_colliders_blockable` formalises the LN remark "every non-collider on a walk in an acyclic CDMG is blockable" as a per-walk-per-position implication. Its binder list reads `(G : CDMG Node) (hG : G.IsAcyclic) {u v : Node} (π : Walk G u v) (k : ℕ)`, mirroring the LN's layered "let $G$ be a CDMG, suppose $G$ is acyclic, then for every walk $\pi$ … every position $k$…".

`Node : Type*` is the ambient vertex universe — the LN's silent assumption that $G$ ranges over some fixed carrier $J \cup V$. The `[DecidableEq Node]` instance, introduced separately via a `variable` line, is the typing infrastructure that the per-position predicates `IsNonCollider` (from `def_3_15`) and `IsBlockableNonCollider` (from `def_3_16`) require.

`G : CDMG Node` is the bundled CDMG structure; the LN's components $(J, V, E, L)$ live as fields `G.J`, `G.V`, `G.E`, `G.L`. The acyclicity assumption `hG : G.IsAcyclic` is the propositional `def_3_6` predicate — stated as a separate hypothesis rather than baked into the type of `G`. The pair `{u v : Node}` is made *implicit* because both endpoints are recoverable from the walk's type `Walk G u v`; this matches the binder shape used by `IsBlockableNonCollider` and keeps the theorem ergonomic for dot-notation callers like `π.IsNonCollider k`.

`Walk G u v` is the inductive type of `def_3_4` item i. walks; it carries its source and target in its type, and is built by `nil` / `cons` constructors. The conclusion `π.IsNonCollider k → π.IsBlockableNonCollider k` reuses the two per-position predicates *character-for-character* from `def_3_15` and `def_3_16`. By the latter, `IsBlockableNonCollider` unfolds to `IsNonCollider ∧ ¬ IsUnblockableNonCollider`, so the conclusion structurally re-asserts the antecedent in its first conjunct — a deliberate redundancy discussed below.

In shape, the theorem takes a CDMG, an acyclicity hypothesis, a walk, and a position, and concludes the per-position implication. No `k ≤ π.length` side condition appears: out-of-range positions make the antecedent definitionally `False`, so the implication holds vacuously.
design_choices:
**The CDMG is typed; acyclicity is propositional.** The LN reads "let $G$ be a CDMG and suppose $G$ is acyclic". The formalization splits these asymmetrically: CDMG-hood is enforced at the type level by `G : CDMG Node` (no separate hypothesis), while acyclicity is carried by the opt-in propositional hypothesis `hG : G.IsAcyclic` using `def_3_6`'s predicate verbatim. This mirrors how the rest of §3.3 composes hypotheses — most per-position predicates take a bare `CDMG` and do *not* assume acyclicity, so making acyclicity an explicit `Prop`-level binder keeps the theorem maximally compatible with the broader section. Bundling both into a typeclass `[AcyclicCDMG Node G]` or into a structured sub-type was rejected because it would force every downstream consumer of the per-position predicates to re-derive the bare CDMG view.

**Conclusion as an implication, not as a conjunction.** The LN says "(non-collider) $\implies$ (blockable non-collider)". The formalization transports this implication-in-conclusion shape word-for-word. A leaner variant `IsNonCollider k → ¬ IsUnblockableNonCollider k` would drop the definitional redundancy (since `IsBlockableNonCollider` already contains `IsNonCollider` as a conjunct) but force every downstream consumer to re-assemble the LN-named predicate by hand. Keeping the conclusion as `IsBlockableNonCollider` lets a caller with the non-collider hypothesis in scope obtain the LN-named predicate in a single step, without unfolding `def_3_16`.

**Per-walk-per-position quantification at the binder level.** The two per-position predicates `IsNonCollider` and `IsBlockableNonCollider` are both indexed by `(p, k)`. The theorem mirrors this verbatim: the binders introduce `(π, k)` directly, and the conclusion is the per-position implication. The alternative `∀ π k, π.IsNonCollider k → π.IsBlockableNonCollider k` form, pushed *inside* the conclusion, would be logically equivalent but force consumers to apply the explicit quantifier before reading off the implication — breaking the chapter-wide binder-level convention.

**Implicit walk endpoints `{u v : Node}`.** Since `Walk G u v` carries its source and target in its type, the endpoints are always recoverable from any walk and never need to be named at the call site. Making them implicit reproduces the binder shape of `IsBlockableNonCollider` and keeps the theorem ergonomically compatible with dot-notation.

**No explicit position bound.** The LN scopes $k \in \{0, \dots, n\}$; the formalization carries this implicitly. `IsNonCollider`'s own in-range conjunct already enforces $k \le \pi.\text{length}$, so out-of-range positions make the antecedent `False` and the implication holds vacuously. An explicit `k ≤ π.length` hypothesis would be redundant.
